title: The Instruction Tuning Milestone
concept: PHASE D4 COMPLETE. We have mastered base-to-instruct conversion, SFT loss masking, and instruction following evaluation. Boss explain ends with: 'Phase D4: Instruction Tuning is complete. Next up: RLHF & Alignment.'

exercises.prompt: What is the primary goal of instruction tuning?

options.text: To make the model use less electricity.
To teach a base model to follow instructions and behave like an assistant.
To translate the model's code into different programming languages.

exercises.explain: Instruction tuning is the key step that transforms a raw base text predictor into an interactive, conversational assistant.

cards.title: From Autocomplete to Assistant
cards.body: Raw language models are great at autocomplete, but terrible at taking orders! To turn a raw text predictor into a helpful chatbot assistant, we need a special training step called Instruction Tuning. Let's see how we bridged this gap.

cards.title: What is Instruction Tuning?
cards.body: Instruction tuning (or Supervised Fine-Tuning) is the process of taking a base model trained on raw internet text and training it further on formatted prompt-response pairs. This teaches the model to behave like a helpful assistant rather than just predicting the next word on a random web page.

cards.title: The Study Guide Analogy
cards.body: Think of a raw base LLM as a brilliant student who has read the entire library but doesn't know how a school test works. Instruction tuning is like giving them practice exams with sample question-and-answer pairs so they learn how to format their knowledge into helpful answers.

cards.title: Base vs. Instruct Behavior
cards.body:
Instead of completing a sentence like:
'Write a poem about a cat. A cat is...'
An instruction-tuned model reads:
'User: Write a poem about a cat. Assistant:'
And correctly responds with:
'Whiskers soft and eyes so bright, chasing shadows in the night...'

cards.title: Myth: It Teaches New Facts
cards.body: Many people think instruction tuning teaches the model brand new facts. In reality, the model already knows the facts from its base training! Instruction tuning simply teaches the model the *format* of how to behave, listen, and respond.

cards.title: Base vs. Instruct Comparison
cards.body: Let's compare the behavior of a Base Model versus an Instruction-Tuned (Instruct) Model when given a command.

left.text: Input: 'Write a cake recipe.' -> Output: 'Making a cake is easy. You need flour, sugar...' (Autocompletes the text style)

right.text: Input: 'Write a cake recipe.' -> Output: 'Here is a simple recipe: 1. Preheat oven... 2. Mix ingredients...' (Answers the request)

exercises.prompt: Why do we mask the user prompt tokens during Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) loss calculation?

options.text: To make the model run faster during inference.
To only grade the model on its generated answers, not the user's prompt.
To hide secret data from the model.
To prevent the model from learning English.

exercises.explain: SFT loss masking ensures the model is only graded on how well it generates the answer, not how well it predicts the user's question.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A base model and an instruction-tuned model are trained on different formats of data.
exercises.explain: A base model is trained on unformatted raw internet text, whereas an instruct model is specifically trained on user-assistant dialog formats.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete how we calculate loss during instruction tuning.
exercises.sentence: During SFT training, we mask the prompt tokens and only calculate the loss on the model's response tokens.
exercises.explain: We mask the user prompt tokens and only calculate the loss on the assistant's response tokens so the model learns how to answer, not how to write prompts.

exercises.prompt: Which model has successfully undergone instruction tuning?

options.text: AI Chatbot A: "Translate 'Hello' to Spanish: Spanish is a Romance language spoken by millions."
AI Chatbot B: "Translate 'Hello' to Spanish: Hola."

exercises.explain: AI Chatbot B shows proper instruction-following behavior by directly answering the user's prompt, while AI Chatbot A simply autocompletes the text.

exercises.prompt: To successfully complete Phase D4 (Instruction Tuning), which sequence of steps did we master?

options.text: Train the model on internet text without any templates.
Use RLHF to adjust safety parameters before doing SFT.
Mask prompt tokens during SFT, calculate loss on response tokens, and evaluate using automated benchmarks.
Increase the learning rate on the prompt tokens during training.

exercises.explain: Phase D4: Instruction Tuning is complete. Next up: RLHF & Alignment.
